mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions drive wage growth and consumer spending, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with industrial production above 100 confirm broad-based economic expansion. This joint signal drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate capex and consumer demand accelerate.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly positive when both proxies trigger simultaneously, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer credit balances alongside rising inflation compress real disposable income, triggering negative equity returns over the next week as consumer spending decelerates and margin pressure mounts.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of triggered instances despite rising credit and inflation, or the joint signal shows no statistical edge over a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and aggregate supply. This supply-side expansion historically correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as output growth supports earnings.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 5.0 percent signal a restrictive monetary stance that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm labor market tightening. This joint signal drives immediate positive equity momentum over the next week as wage pressures boost nominal spending.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both PAYEMS and UNRATE move favorably.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions where GDP growth exceeds 3 percent annualized and housing starts remain elevated, equity markets experience sustained positive returns over the next month as corporate profits peak.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDP growth decelerates below 2.5 percent annualized despite high housing starts, or the signal loses predictive power in recessionary periods.
